scenarios.opening_query: My disability hearing got continued twice and now my landlord won't wait any longer.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's my SSDI case — 23 years of grocery-warehouse wages back my claim.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's SSDI — 23 years of warehouse wages behind it.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's SSI — I've been unable to work since my teens.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's SSI — I haven't been able to work since my teens.

scenarios.opening_query: The disability people say my husband's pension counts against me somehow and cut my check in half.
scenarios.hidden_fact: Only SSI counts a spouse's income — mine is the SSI check, and the deeming calculation is what needs fighting.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my SSI — only SSI counts a spouse's income, and their deeming math is wrong.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether spousal deeming (SSI-only) explains the cut
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my SSDI — a spouse's pension shouldn't touch it at all, which is why their letter makes no sense and needs appealing.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's SSDI — his pension shouldn't touch it at all. The letter's just wrong.
scenarios.vetting_note: The rule itself identifies the program.

scenarios.opening_query: I care for my disabled sister full time and her benefits case has become my second job.
scenarios.hidden_fact: Her claim is Disabled Adult Child benefits on our father's earnings record — the SSDI side, through his work history.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Hers is the disabled-adult-child benefit on Dad's record — the SSDI side.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whose earnings record supports the claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Hers is SSI — needs-based, with the annual redetermination paperwork drowning us.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Hers is SSI — needs-based, and the redetermination paperwork is drowning us.

scenarios.opening_query: After my transplant I can't return to my old work and the denial letter felt like an insult.
scenarios.hidden_fact: I paid in for 19 years as a press operator — the claim rides my own record.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Nineteen years as a press operator — it's on my record.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: I was uninsured for SSDI — too many gap years caregiving — so mine is the SSI claim.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Too many caregiving gap years — mine's the SSI claim.

scenarios.opening_query: My veteran son gets a small government check but everyone says he qualifies for more than he receives.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Administrative Law > SSD || Administrative Law > Social Security/SSI || Administrative Law > Military/Veterans
scenarios.hidden_fact: The small check is SSI — his service was too short for VA pension or SSDI credits, and maximizing the SSI side with state supplements is the question.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The check is SSI — service too short for VA pension or SSDI credits. Maximizing SSI with state supplements is the question.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: which benefit stream the veteran actually receives
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The check is VA disability at 30% — pushing his rating higher through the VA is the fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's VA disability at 30% — the rating increase is the fight.

scenarios.opening_query: The disability review board wants me examined by their doctor for the third time in two years.
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's my SSDI from my machinist years — and the repeat exams started right after my benefit amount was corrected upward.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my SSDI from machining — and the repeat exams started right after my benefit got corrected upward.
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's SSI — and each 'exam' also re-runs my finances, which is the SSI redetermination machine.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's SSI — every exam re-runs my finances too.
